summary: Discogs API market valuation integration testing - ALL BACKEND TESTS PASSED (19/19). All valuation features are working correctly. Fixed a bug in DiscogsSearchResult model where format field expected string but Discogs returns list.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/valuation/collection requires auth (401)
Backend: GET /api/valuation/hidden-gems requires auth (401)
Backend: GET /api/valuation/taste-report requires auth (401)
Backend: GET /api/valuation/record-values requires auth (401)
Backend: GET /api/valuation/pricing-assist/{id} requires auth (401)
Backend: PUT /api/valuation/wantlist/{id}/price-alert requires auth (401)
Backend: POST /api/valuation/refresh requires auth (401)
Backend: GET /api/valuation/collection/demo returns public collection value with total_value, valued_count, total_count
Backend: GET /api/valuation/collection/{nonexistent} returns 404
Backend: GET /api/valuation/hidden-gems/demo returns top 3 most valuable records with median_value
Backend: GET /api/valuation/hidden-gems/{nonexistent} returns 404
Backend: GET /api/valuation/collection (authenticated) returns user's collection value
Backend: GET /api/valuation/hidden-gems (authenticated) returns top 3 sorted by value descending
Backend: GET /api/valuation/taste-report returns total_value, most_valuable, over_100_count, valued_count
Backend: GET /api/valuation/record-values returns record_id -> median_value map
Backend: GET /api/valuation/pricing-assist/249504 returns low, high, median prices
Backend: POST /api/valuation/refresh triggers background refresh
Backend: PUT /api/valuation/wantlist/{invalid}/price-alert returns 404
Backend: PUT /api/valuation/wantlist/{valid_iso}/price-alert sets target_price correctly
Frontend: Collection page shows estimated value banner with $7,603
Frontend: Collection page shows Hidden Gems section with top 3 records
Frontend: Collection page shows Taste Report card (total value, most valuable, 13 records over $100)
Frontend: Collection page has Refresh values button
Frontend: Collection page record cards show value badges ($381, $228, $199, etc.)
Frontend: Collection page has 'Highest Value' sort option working
Frontend: Profile page shows 'Est. Value' stat ($7,603) next to Records/Following/Followers
Frontend: The Honeypot listing modal shows pricing assist 'recent sales: $10.00 — $38.00 on Discogs'
Frontend: Wantlist items show 'Set price alert' link that expands to input field
Frontend: Setting price alert shows badge 'Alert at $50' on ISO card
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created - using existing demo user collection with 155 records, 143 valued
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Discogs valuation features are fully working. Demo user has 155 records with 143 valued at $7,603 total. Top 3 hidden gems: Taylor Swift - Lover (Live From Paris) at $381, Folklore Long Pond at $228, Ariana Grande - Sweetener at $199. 13 records valued over $100. Pricing assist API works with Discogs release IDs. Wantlist price alerts work on ISO items. Fixed bug in DiscogsSearchResult model - format field is now List[str] instead of str.

bugs_fixed.issue: DiscogsSearchResult.format field expected string but Discogs API returns list
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed format field type from Optional[str] to Optional[List[str]]

collection_value.ui_location: Collection page banner + Profile page stat

hidden_gems.ui_location: Collection page section showing top 3 most valuable records

taste_report.ui_location: Collection page card (dark bg) with total value, most valuable, over $100 count

record_values.ui_location: Value badges on record cards in Collection page

pricing_assist.ui_location: Honeypot listing modal - shows 'recent sales: $X — $Y on Discogs' below price input when Discogs release selected

wantlist_price_alerts.ui_location: ISO tab - 'Set price alert' link expands to input, shows badge after setting
